{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_8353f32f3b6b43baa4ef950c","type":"tool_result","content":"0\t# Miroir — Implementation Plan\n1\t\n2\t**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n3\t\n4\tMiroir is a RAID-like sharding and high-availability layer for Meilisearch Community Edition. It stripes a large index across a fleet of Meilisearch nodes, fans out search queries across all shards, merges ranked results, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all without Meilisearch Enterprise.\n5\t\n6\t---\n7\t\n8\t## Table of Contents\n9\t\n10\t1. [Design Principles](#1-design-principles)\n11\t2. [Architecture](#2-architecture)\n12\t3. [Document, Index, and Search Lifecycle](#3-document-index-and-search-lifecycle)\n13\t4. [Implementation](#4-implementation)\n14\t5. [API Surface and Compatibility](#5-api-surface-and-compatibility)\n15\t6. [Deployment](#6-deployment)\n16\t7. [CI/CD](#7-cicd)\n17\t8. [Testing](#8-testing)\n18\t9. [Secrets Handling](#9-secrets-handling)\n19\t10. [Observability](#10-observability)\n20\t11. [Onboarding](#11-onboarding)\n21\t12. [Delivered Artifacts](#12-delivered-artifacts)\n22\t13. [Advanced Capabilities](#13-advanced-capabilities)\n23\t14. [Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling](#14-resource-envelope-and-horizontal-scaling)\n24\t15. [Open Problems](#15-open-problems)\n25\t\n26\t---\n27\t\n28\t## 1. Design Principles\n29\t\n30\t1. **Invisible federation** — clients talk to one endpoint using the standard Meilisearch API. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n31\t2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n32\t3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n33\t4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n34\t5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n35\t6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n36\t7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n37\t8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n38\t\n39\t### Key constraints\n40\t\n41\t- **Logical shard count (S) is fixed at index creation** — this is the granularity of the hash space, not the number of physical machines. It determines how finely the keyspace is divided. Changing S requires a full reindex. Choose generously, per group: `S = max_nodes_per_group_ever × 8`. S is scoped to a single replica group because rendezvous assignment runs within each group independently (see Section 2); adding groups multiplies throughput without consuming additional S headroom.\n42\t- **Node count (N) is fully elastic** — nodes can be added or removed at any time without a full reindex. Adding a node to a group migrates only the `~1/(Ng+1)` fraction of that group's documents whose shard assignments change, where Ng is the number of nodes in the affected group. Documents in other groups are untouched. Removing a node migrates only that node's documents to surviving nodes within its own group. The rest of the corpus is untouched.\n43\t- All Meilisearch nodes must have **identical index settings** at all times. Miroir enforces this by broadcasting settings atomically.\n44\t- Documents **must** have an explicit primary key declared at index creation. Miroir cannot shard-route without it.\n45\t\n46\t---\n47\t\n48\t## 2. Architecture\n49\t\n50\t### Core model\n51\t\n52\tMiroir treats a fleet of Meilisearch CE instances the way software RAID treats a disk array, with an additional dimension for read throughput: **replica groups**.\n53\t\n54\t- The logical index is divided into **S shards** — a fixed hash-space granularity chosen at index creation\n55\t- The node fleet is divided into **RG replica groups** — independent pools each holding a complete copy of all shards\n56\t- Within each group, each shard is held by **RF nodes** (intra-group replication factor, for HA within the group)\n57\t- Every write fans out to all `RG × RF` target nodes (one per shard per group replica)\n58\t- Every search query is routed to exactly **one replica group** — the query fans out only to that group's covering set, not the entire fleet\n59\t- Read throughput scales with RG: doubling the groups doubles query capacity\n60\t- Storage capacity scales with total nodes: adding a node to a group reduces each node's share within that group\n61\t- The **node fleet is elastic**: nodes can be added to existing groups (capacity scaling) or new groups can be added (throughput scaling), both without a full reindex\n62\t- When the fleet topology changes, the orchestrator rebalances only the affected shards within the affected group\n63\t\n64\t```\n65\tClient\n66\t  │\n67\t  ▼\n68\tMiroir Orchestrator\n69\t  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → shard → fan out to RF nodes in EACH group (RG × RF total)\n70\t  ├── Read path:  pick one group → scatter to that group's covering set → merge results\n71\t  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove within a group → migrate minimum shards within that group\n72\t\n73\t  Replica Group 0              Replica Group 1\n74\t  ┌──────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐\n75\t  │ node-0  node-1   │        │ node-3  node-4   │     ...  (RG groups)\n76\t  │ node-2           │        │ node-5           │\n77\t  └──────────────────┘        └──────────────────┘\n78\t  (each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes)\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### Replication factor\n82\t\n83\t| RF | Redundancy | Node failures tolerated | Capacity |\n84\t|----|-----------|------------------------|----------|\n85\t| 1  | None (stripe only) | 0 | 100% of fleet |\n86\t| 2  | One replica | 1 per shard group | 50% of fleet |\n87\t| 3  | Two replicas | 2 per shard group | 33% of fleet |\n88\t\n89\t### Shard routing — Rendezvous hashing (HRW) within groups\n90\t\n91\tMiroir uses **Rendezvous hashing (HRW)** to assign shards to nodes. The assignment is scoped **within each replica group** — the rendezvous hash picks the top-RF nodes from a group's node list, not the global node list. This guarantees that every group independently forms a complete covering set with no cross-group shard overlap.\n92\t\n93\t```\n94\tnodes_in_group(g)           → the subset of nodes belonging to replica group g\n95\tassign_shard(shard_id, g)   → top RF nodes from nodes_in_group(g)\n96\t                              by score(hash(shard_id || node_id))\n97\t\n98\twrite_targets(shard_id)     → [assign_shard(shard_id, g) for g in 0..RG]\n99\t                              (flat union across all groups — RG × RF nodes total)\n100\t\n101\tsearch_group(query_seq)     → query_seq % RG   (round-robin across groups)\n102\tsearch_nodes(query)         → covering_set within search_group(query)\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\tWhy group-scoped assignment matters: if rendezvous ran over the global node list, both replicas of a shard might land on nodes in the same group, leaving the other group without coverage of that shard. Scoping to the group prevents this.\n106\t\n107\tProperties:\n108\t- On node addition within a group: only `~1/(Ng+1)` of that group's documents migrate, where Ng = nodes per group\n109\t- On node removal within a group: only `~RF/Ng` of that group's documents migrate\n110\t- Both operations leave other groups and all unaffected shards completely untouched\n111\t- No virtual nodes needed — rendezvous is naturally uniform\n112\t- Deterministic — any component can compute assignments from the same node list; no coordination required\n113\t\n114\tThe hash function is **twox-hash (xxhash family)** — the same function Meilisearch Enterprise uses for document sharding.\n115\t\n116\t### Write path\n117\t\n118\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/documents` with a batch of documents\n119\t2. Orchestrator extracts the document primary key for each document\n120\t3. For each document: `shard_id = hash(doc_id) % S`\n121\t4. **Inject `_miroir_shard`**: add `\"_miroir_shard\": shard_id` to each document before forwarding. This field is stored on the node and used during rebalancing to retrieve documents by shard without a full scan. It is stripped from all API responses before returning to clients.\n122\t5. For each replica group g in `0..RG`: compute `assign_shard(shard_id, g)` → the RF nodes within that group that own this shard\n123\t6. Fan out: send each document to all `RG × RF` target nodes in parallel (grouped into per-node batches)\n124\t7. Apply the two-rule quorum described below; set `X-Miroir-Degraded` on any group that missed its per-group quorum\n125\t8. Return Miroir task ID to client\n126\t\n127\t**Write quorum — two rules.**\n128\t\n129\t1. **Per-group quorum.** A group is successful for a shard when at least `floor(RF/2) + 1` of its RF target nodes ACK enqueue. (RF=1 → 1 ACK; RF=2 → 2 ACKs; RF=3 → 2 ACKs; RF=4 → 3 ACKs.)\n130\t2. **Write success.** The write returns HTTP 200 if **at least one group** met its per-group quorum for the shard. The response carries `X-Miroir-Degraded` whenever **any** group did not meet its quorum for any shard in the batch (listing the affected groups and shards). HTTP 503 with `miroir_no_quorum` is returned only when **no group** met its per-group quorum for a given shard.\n131\t\n132\tExamples:\n133\t\n134\t| Topology | Per-group quorum | Write succeeds when |\n135\t|----------|------------------|---------------------|\n136\t| RG=1, RF=1 | 1 of 1 | that one node ACKs |\n137\t| RG=1, RF=2 | 2 of 2 | both replicas ACK |\n138\t| RG=1, RF=3 | 2 of 3 | any 2 of the 3 replicas ACK |\n139\t| RG=2, RF=1 | 1 of 1 per group | at least one of the two groups' single node ACKs |\n140\t| RG=2, RF=3 | 2 of 3 per group | either group reaches 2-of-3 ACKs |\n141\t\n142\tDocuments whose replicas did not ACK are not lost: any group that accepted the write becomes the authoritative source, and the anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) subsequently repairs any replica that missed the write on the next pass. This composition — best-effort fan-out, per-shard success when at least one group reaches quorum, plus continuous reconciliation — is the long-term safety net and the concrete expression of Design Principle 5 (graceful degradation) for the write path. This not-lost guarantee depends on `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8, default on). When anti-entropy is disabled, writes that don't reach every replica remain permanently divergent; operators running with anti-entropy off must either require full-replica-set writes (e.g., stricter client retries) or provide their own reconciliation.\n143\t\n144\t**`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name**: clients must not use a field with this name. Miroir rejects any document that already contains `_miroir_shard` with a `miroir_reserved_field` error.\n145\t\n146\t**Index settings and schema changes**: broadcast to all nodes in all groups. Index creation additionally broadcasts a settings update to add `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes` on every node — this is required for efficient rebalancing.\n147\t\n148\t### Read path\n149\t\n150\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/search`\n151\t2. Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\n152\t3. Within the selected group, build a **covering set**: one node per shard (round-robin across intra-group replicas for load balancing within the group)\n153\t4. Fan out the query to each node in the covering set in parallel, with `showRankingScore: true` appended\n154\t5. Each node executes a full Meilisearch search against its local document subset\n155\t6. **Result merge**: collect all hits, sort globally by `_rankingScore` descending, apply original `offset`/`limit`, strip `_rankingScore` if client did not request it, strip `_miroir_shard` always\n156\t7. **Facets**: sum per-value counts across the covering set nodes\n157\t8. **estimatedTotalHits**: sum across covering set nodes\n158\t9. **processingTimeMs**: report max across covering set nodes\n159\t\n160\tThe query only touches `N/RG` nodes (the nodes in the selected group), not the entire fleet. Adding more groups reduces the fan-out size per query and increases the number of queries that can run in parallel across the fleet.\n161\t\n162\t**Score comparability**: `_rankingScore` values are comparable across shards only if all nodes have identical ranking rules, typo tolerance, attribute weights, and synonyms. Miroir enforces this via the settings broadcast requirement. Because all groups receive identical settings changes, inter-group score comparability is also maintained (relevant for degraded-mode fallback, below).\n163\t\n164\t**Group unavailability fallback**: if the selected group has an unhealthy node and the shard's intra-group RF cannot cover it, Miroir can optionally fall back to a different group for that query. The result is still complete and correct — it just came from a different group. This is transparent to the client.\n165\t\n166\t**offset/limit**: each node must return up to `offset + limit` results. The orchestrator applies the real offset/limit after global merge.\n167\t\n168\t### Replica groups in practice\n169\t\n170\tReplica groups make read throughput a first-class scaling dimension, independent of storage capacity.\n171\t\n172\t```\n173\tExample: S=6 shards, RG=2 groups, RF=1 per group, 6 nodes total\n174\t\n175\tGroup 0: nodes [0, 1, 2]          Group 1: nodes [3, 4, 5]\n176\t  shard 0 → node 0                  shard 0 → node 3\n177\t  shard 1 → node 1                  shard 1 → node 4\n178\t  shard 2 → node 2                  shard 2 → node 5\n179\t  shard 3 → node 0                  shard 3 → node 3\n180\t  shard 4 → node 1                  shard 4 → node 4\n181\t  shard 5 → node 2                  shard 5 → node 5\n182\t\n183\tWrite for doc-X (shard 2): → node-2 (group 0) AND node-5 (group 1)  [2 writes]\n184\tQuery 1: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [3 node fan-out]\n185\tQuery 2: → group 1 → nodes [3, 4, 5]   [3 node fan-out]\n186\tQuery 3: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [queries 1 and 3 run fully in parallel]\n187\t```\n188\t\n189\tWith RG=2, two queries can execute simultaneously with zero resource contention. Adding a third group triples throughput, and so on.\n190\t\n191\t**Tradeoffs table**\n192\t\n193\t| RG | RF | Copies/doc | Read throughput | Storage per node | HA (within group) |\n194\t|----|-----|-----------|----------------|-----------------|------------------|\n195\t| 1 | 1 | 1 | 1× | full | none |\n196\t| 1 | 2 | 2 | 1× (replicas share load) | half | 1 node failure |\n197\t| 1 | 3 | 3 | 1× | third | 2 node failures |\n198\t| 2 | 1 | 2 | **2×** | half per group | none |\n199\t| 2 | 2 | 4 | **2×** | quarter per group | 1 node failure per group |"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d81bc991-fb6d-41e4-96b1-c6a84f8f541a","uuid":"9bc73449-32d2-475f-a8f6-124c7d36c537","timestamp":"2026-05-13T23:04:34.968Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"# Miroir — Implementation Plan\n\n**Multi-node Index Replication Orchestrator, Integrated Rebalancing**\n\nMiroir is a RAID-like sharding and high-availability layer for Meilisearch Community Edition. It stripes a large index across a fleet of Meilisearch nodes, fans out search queries across all shards, merges ranked results, and rebalances shard assignments when nodes are added or removed — all without Meilisearch Enterprise.\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [Open Problems](#15-open-problems)\n\n---\n\n## 1. Design Principles\n\n1. **Invisible federation** — clients talk to one endpoint using the standard Meilisearch API. The sharding topology is entirely hidden. No SDK changes, no query changes, no schema changes.\n2. **No Enterprise dependency** — all functionality is built on Meilisearch Community Edition (MIT licensed).\n3. **Rendezvous hashing** — same algorithm Meilisearch Enterprise uses internally; minimal reshuffling on topology change, natural RF > 1 support.\n4. **RF-configurable redundancy** — RF=1 for maximum capacity, RF=2 for one-node-loss tolerance, RF=3 for two-node-loss tolerance.\n5. **Graceful degradation** — when a shard is unavailable, return partial results with a clear header rather than failing the entire request.\n6. **Static binaries, scratch images** — musl compilation + scratch Docker base for minimal attack surface and trivial deployment.\n7. **GitOps first** — all deployment configuration committed to `jedarden/declarative-config`; ArgoCD drives all cluster changes.\n8. **Fixed per-pod resource envelope** — each Miroir orchestrator pod fits within **2 vCPU / 3.75 GB RAM**. When aggregate workload exceeds this envelope, scale **horizontally** by adding pods, never vertically beyond the envelope. The request path is strictly stateless; background work partitions across pods via shard-partitioned ownership, leader election, or a shared job queue. See Section 14.\n\n### Key constraints\n\n- **Logical shard count (S) is fixed at index creation** — this is the granularity of the hash space, not the number of physical machines. It determines how finely the keyspace is divided. Changing S requires a full reindex. Choose generously, per group: `S = max_nodes_per_group_ever × 8`. S is scoped to a single replica group because rendezvous assignment runs within each group independently (see Section 2); adding groups multiplies throughput without consuming additional S headroom.\n- **Node count (N) is fully elastic** — nodes can be added or removed at any time without a full reindex. Adding a node to a group migrates only the `~1/(Ng+1)` fraction of that group's documents whose shard assignments change, where Ng is the number of nodes in the affected group. Documents in other groups are untouched. Removing a node migrates only that node's documents to surviving nodes within its own group. The rest of the corpus is untouched.\n- All Meilisearch nodes must have **identical index settings** at all times. Miroir enforces this by broadcasting settings atomically.\n- Documents **must** have an explicit primary key declared at index creation. Miroir cannot shard-route without it.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Architecture\n\n### Core model\n\nMiroir treats a fleet of Meilisearch CE instances the way software RAID treats a disk array, with an additional dimension for read throughput: **replica groups**.\n\n- The logical index is divided into **S shards** — a fixed hash-space granularity chosen at index creation\n- The node fleet is divided into **RG replica groups** — independent pools each holding a complete copy of all shards\n- Within each group, each shard is held by **RF nodes** (intra-group replication factor, for HA within the group)\n- Every write fans out to all `RG × RF` target nodes (one per shard per group replica)\n- Every search query is routed to exactly **one replica group** — the query fans out only to that group's covering set, not the entire fleet\n- Read throughput scales with RG: doubling the groups doubles query capacity\n- Storage capacity scales with total nodes: adding a node to a group reduces each node's share within that group\n- The **node fleet is elastic**: nodes can be added to existing groups (capacity scaling) or new groups can be added (throughput scaling), both without a full reindex\n- When the fleet topology changes, the orchestrator rebalances only the affected shards within the affected group\n\n```\nClient\n  │\n  ▼\nMiroir Orchestrator\n  ├── Write path: hash(doc_id) → shard → fan out to RF nodes in EACH group (RG × RF total)\n  ├── Read path:  pick one group → scatter to that group's covering set → merge results\n  └── Rebalance: on node add/remove within a group → migrate minimum shards within that group\n\n  Replica Group 0              Replica Group 1\n  ┌──────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐\n  │ node-0  node-1   │        │ node-3  node-4   │     ...  (RG groups)\n  │ node-2           │        │ node-5           │\n  └──────────────────┘        └──────────────────┘\n  (each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes)\n```\n\n### Replication factor\n\n| RF | Redundancy | Node failures tolerated | Capacity |\n|----|-----------|------------------------|----------|\n| 1  | None (stripe only) | 0 | 100% of fleet |\n| 2  | One replica | 1 per shard group | 50% of fleet |\n| 3  | Two replicas | 2 per shard group | 33% of fleet |\n\n### Shard routing — Rendezvous hashing (HRW) within groups\n\nMiroir uses **Rendezvous hashing (HRW)** to assign shards to nodes. The assignment is scoped **within each replica group** — the rendezvous hash picks the top-RF nodes from a group's node list, not the global node list. This guarantees that every group independently forms a complete covering set with no cross-group shard overlap.\n\n```\nnodes_in_group(g)           → the subset of nodes belonging to replica group g\nassign_shard(shard_id, g)   → top RF nodes from nodes_in_group(g)\n                              by score(hash(shard_id || node_id))\n\nwrite_targets(shard_id)     → [assign_shard(shard_id, g) for g in 0..RG]\n                              (flat union across all groups — RG × RF nodes total)\n\nsearch_group(query_seq)     → query_seq % RG   (round-robin across groups)\nsearch_nodes(query)         → covering_set within search_group(query)\n```\n\nWhy group-scoped assignment matters: if rendezvous ran over the global node list, both replicas of a shard might land on nodes in the same group, leaving the other group without coverage of that shard. Scoping to the group prevents this.\n\nProperties:\n- On node addition within a group: only `~1/(Ng+1)` of that group's documents migrate, where Ng = nodes per group\n- On node removal within a group: only `~RF/Ng` of that group's documents migrate\n- Both operations leave other groups and all unaffected shards completely untouched\n- No virtual nodes needed — rendezvous is naturally uniform\n- Deterministic — any component can compute assignments from the same node list; no coordination required\n\nThe hash function is **twox-hash (xxhash family)** — the same function Meilisearch Enterprise uses for document sharding.\n\n### Write path\n\n1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/documents` with a batch of documents\n2. Orchestrator extracts the document primary key for each document\n3. For each document: `shard_id = hash(doc_id) % S`\n4. **Inject `_miroir_shard`**: add `\"_miroir_shard\": shard_id` to each document before forwarding. This field is stored on the node and used during rebalancing to retrieve documents by shard without a full scan. It is stripped from all API responses before returning to clients.\n5. For each replica group g in `0..RG`: compute `assign_shard(shard_id, g)` → the RF nodes within that group that own this shard\n6. Fan out: send each document to all `RG × RF` target nodes in parallel (grouped into per-node batches)\n7. Apply the two-rule quorum described below; set `X-Miroir-Degraded` on any group that missed its per-group quorum\n8. Return Miroir task ID to client\n\n**Write quorum — two rules.**\n\n1. **Per-group quorum.** A group is successful for a shard when at least `floor(RF/2) + 1` of its RF target nodes ACK enqueue. (RF=1 → 1 ACK; RF=2 → 2 ACKs; RF=3 → 2 ACKs; RF=4 → 3 ACKs.)\n2. **Write success.** The write returns HTTP 200 if **at least one group** met its per-group quorum for the shard. The response carries `X-Miroir-Degraded` whenever **any** group did not meet its quorum for any shard in the batch (listing the affected groups and shards). HTTP 503 with `miroir_no_quorum` is returned only when **no group** met its per-group quorum for a given shard.\n\nExamples:\n\n| Topology | Per-group quorum | Write succeeds when |\n|----------|------------------|---------------------|\n| RG=1, RF=1 | 1 of 1 | that one node ACKs |\n| RG=1, RF=2 | 2 of 2 | both replicas ACK |\n| RG=1, RF=3 | 2 of 3 | any 2 of the 3 replicas ACK |\n| RG=2, RF=1 | 1 of 1 per group | at least one of the two groups' single node ACKs |\n| RG=2, RF=3 | 2 of 3 per group | either group reaches 2-of-3 ACKs |\n\nDocuments whose replicas did not ACK are not lost: any group that accepted the write becomes the authoritative source, and the anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) subsequently repairs any replica that missed the write on the next pass. This composition — best-effort fan-out, per-shard success when at least one group reaches quorum, plus continuous reconciliation — is the long-term safety net and the concrete expression of Design Principle 5 (graceful degradation) for the write path. This not-lost guarantee depends on `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8, default on). When anti-entropy is disabled, writes that don't reach every replica remain permanently divergent; operators running with anti-entropy off must either require full-replica-set writes (e.g., stricter client retries) or provide their own reconciliation.\n\n**`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name**: clients must not use a field with this name. Miroir rejects any document that already contains `_miroir_shard` with a `miroir_reserved_field` error.\n\n**Index settings and schema changes**: broadcast to all nodes in all groups. Index creation additionally broadcasts a settings update to add `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes` on every node — this is required for efficient rebalancing.\n\n### Read path\n\n1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/search`\n2. Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\n3. Within the selected group, build a **covering set**: one node per shard (round-robin across intra-group replicas for load balancing within the group)\n4. Fan out the query to each node in the covering set in parallel, with `showRankingScore: true` appended\n5. Each node executes a full Meilisearch search against its local document subset\n6. **Result merge**: collect all hits, sort globally by `_rankingScore` descending, apply original `offset`/`limit`, strip `_rankingScore` if client did not request it, strip `_miroir_shard` always\n7. **Facets**: sum per-value counts across the covering set nodes\n8. **estimatedTotalHits**: sum across covering set nodes\n9. **processingTimeMs**: report max across covering set nodes\n\nThe query only touches `N/RG` nodes (the nodes in the selected group), not the entire fleet. Adding more groups reduces the fan-out size per query and increases the number of queries that can run in parallel across the fleet.\n\n**Score comparability**: `_rankingScore` values are comparable across shards only if all nodes have identical ranking rules, typo tolerance, attribute weights, and synonyms. Miroir enforces this via the settings broadcast requirement. Because all groups receive identical settings changes, inter-group score comparability is also maintained (relevant for degraded-mode fallback, below).\n\n**Group unavailability fallback**: if the selected group has an unhealthy node and the shard's intra-group RF cannot cover it, Miroir can optionally fall back to a different group for that query. The result is still complete and correct — it just came from a different group. This is transparent to the client.\n\n**offset/limit**: each node must return up to `offset + limit` results. The orchestrator applies the real offset/limit after global merge.\n\n### Replica groups in practice\n\nReplica groups make read throughput a first-class scaling dimension, independent of storage capacity.\n\n```\nExample: S=6 shards, RG=2 groups, RF=1 per group, 6 nodes total\n\nGroup 0: nodes [0, 1, 2]          Group 1: nodes [3, 4, 5]\n  shard 0 → node 0                  shard 0 → node 3\n  shard 1 → node 1                  shard 1 → node 4\n  shard 2 → node 2                  shard 2 → node 5\n  shard 3 → node 0                  shard 3 → node 3\n  shard 4 → node 1                  shard 4 → node 4\n  shard 5 → node 2                  shard 5 → node 5\n\nWrite for doc-X (shard 2): → node-2 (group 0) AND node-5 (group 1)  [2 writes]\nQuery 1: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [3 node fan-out]\nQuery 2: → group 1 → nodes [3, 4, 5]   [3 node fan-out]\nQuery 3: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [queries 1 and 3 run fully in parallel]\n```\n\nWith RG=2, two queries can execute simultaneously with zero resource contention. Adding a third group triples throughput, and so on.\n\n**Tradeoffs table**\n\n| RG | RF | Copies/doc | Read throughput | Storage per node | HA (within group) |\n|----|-----|-----------|----------------|-----------------|------------------|\n| 1 | 1 | 1 | 1× | full | none |\n| 1 | 2 | 2 | 1× (replicas share load) | half | 1 node failure |\n| 1 | 3 | 3 | 1× | third | 2 node failures |\n| 2 | 1 | 2 | **2×** | half per group | none |\n| 2 | 2 | 4 | **2×** | quarter per group | 1 node failure per group |","numLines":200,"startLine":0,"totalLines":3740}}}

Task store: unified persistence layer for Miroir (plan §4).\n//!\n//! This module provides a trait-based abstraction over two backends:\n//! - SQLite: single-replica, file-based persistence\n//! - Redis: multi-replica, distributed persistence\n//!\n//! Every table in plan §4 is represented here, enabling cross-cutting features\n//! like §13 advanced capabilities and §14 HA mode.\n\nmod error;\nmod redis;\nmod schema;\nmod crate::config::TaskStoreConfig;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\n/// Create a task store based on the provided configuration.\npub async fn create_task_store(config: &TaskStoreConfig) -> Result<Arc<dyn TaskStore>> {\n    match config.backend.as_str() {\n        \"sqlite\" => {\n            let store = SqliteTaskStore::new(&config.path).await?;\n            Ok(Arc::new(store))\n        }\n        \"redis\" => {\n            let store = RedisTaskStore::new(&config.url).await?;\n            Ok(Arc::new(store))\n        }\n        other => Err(TaskStoreError::InvalidBackend(other.to_string())),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Unified task store trait.\n///\n/// All persistence operations flow through this trait, enabling backend\n/// switching at runtime via          |row| row.get(0),\n131\t        )?;\n132\t\n133\t        // Create schema_version table\n134\t        conn.execute(\n135\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (\n136\t                version INTEGER NOT NULL\n137\t            )\",\n138\t            &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n139\t        )?;\n140\t\n141\t        // Check current version\n142\t        let current_version: Option<i64> = conn\n143\t            .query_row(\n144\t                \"SELECT version FROM schema_version\",\n145\t                &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n146\t                |row| row.get(0),\n147\t            )\n148\t            .ok();\n149\t\n150\t        if current_version.is_none() {\n151\t            // Initialize schema\n152\t            Self::init_schema(&conn)?;\n153\t            conn.execute(\n154\t                \"INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (1)\",\n155\t                &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n156\t            )?;\n157\t        } else if current_version != Some(SCHEMA_VERSION) {\n158\t            return Err(TaskStoreError::InvalidData(format!(\n159\t                \"schema version mismatch: expected {}, got {}\",\n160\t                SCHEMA_VERSION,\n161\t                current_version.unwrap()\n162\t            )));\n163\t        }\n164\t\n165\t        Ok(())\n166\t    }\n167\t\n168\t    async fn schema_version(&self) -> Result<i64> {\n169\t        self.query_row(\n170\t            \"SELECT version FROM schema_version\",\n171\t            &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n172\t            |row| row.get(0),\n173\t        )\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    async fn task_insert(&self, task: &Task) -> Result<()> {\n177\t        let node_tasks_json = serde_json::to_string(&task.node_tasks)?;\n178\t        self.execute(\n179\t            \"INSERT INTO tasks (miroir_id, created_at, status, node_tasks, error)\n180\t             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n181\t            &[\n182\t                &task.miroir_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n183\t                &task.created_at,\n184\t                &task.status.to_string(),\n185\t                &node_tasks_json,\n186\t                &task.error.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n187\t            ] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n188\t        )?;\n189\t        Ok(())\n190\t    }\n191\t\n192\t    async fn task_get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Task>> {\n193\t        let result: Option<Task> = self\n194\t            .query_row(\n195\t                \"SELECT miroir_id, created_at,                    Ok(Task {\n201\t                        miroir_id: row.get(0)?,\n202\t                        created_at: row.get(1)?,\n203\t                        status: row.get::<_,            })\n212\t                },\n213\t            )\n214\t            .ok();\n215\t        Ok(result)\n216\t    }\n217\t\n218\t    async fn task_update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: miroir_id: &str, node_id: &str, task_uid: u64) -> Result<()> {\n230\t        // Get the task, update node_tasks (store only task_uid), and write back\n231\t        let mut task = self\n232\t            .task_get(miroir_id)\n233\t            .await?\n234\t            .ok_or_else(|| TaskStoreError::NotFound(miroir_id.to_string()))?;\n235\t        task.node_tasks.insert(node_id.to_string(), task_uid);\n236\t        let node_tasks_json = serde_json::to_string(&task.node_tasks)?;\n237\t        self.execute(\n238\t            \"UPDATE tasks SET node_tasks = ?1 WHERE miroir_id = ?2\",\n239\t            &[\n240\t                &node_tasks_json as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n241\t                &miroir_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n242\t            ],\n243\t        )?;\n244\t        Ok(())\n245\t    }\n246\t\n247\t    async fn task_list(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<Task>> {\n248\t        let mut sql =\n249\t            \"SELECT miroir_id, created_at, status, node_tasks, error FROM tasks\".to_string();\n250\t        let mut params = Vec::new();\n251\t        let mut wheres = Vec::new();\n252\t\n253\t        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n254\t            wheres.push(\"status = ?\".to_string());\n255\t            params.push(status.to_string());\n256\t        }\n257\t\n258\t        if !wheres.is_empty() {\n259\t            sql.push_str(\" WHERE \");\n260\t            sql.push_str(&wheres.join(\" AND \"));\n261\t        }\n262\t\n263\t        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY created_at DESC\");\n264\t\n265\t        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n266\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {limit}\"));\n267\t        }\n268\t\n269\t        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n270\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" OFFSET {offset}\"));\n271\t        }\n272\t\n273\t        let params_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> =\n274\t            params.iter().map(|s| s as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n275\t\n276\t        self.query_map(&sql, &params_refs, |row| {\n277\t            let node_tasks_json: String = row.get(3)?;\n278\t            let node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64> = serde_json::from_str(&node_tasks_json)\n279\t                .map_err(|e| rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n280\t            Ok(Task {\n281\t                miroir_id: row.get(0)?,\n282\t                created_at: row.get(1)?,\n283\t                status: row.get::<_, String>(2)?.parse().map_err(parse_error)?,\n284\t                node_tasks,\n285\t                error: {\n286\t                    let error: String = row.get(4)?;\n287\t                    if error.is_empty() {\n288\t                        None\n289\t                    } else {\n290\t                        Some(error)\n291\t                    }\n292\t                },\n293\t            })\n294\t        })\n295\t    }\n296\t\n297\t    async fn node_settings_version_get(&self, index: index_uid = ?1 AND node_id = ?2\",\n301\t                &[\n302\t                    &index as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n303\t                    &node_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n304\t                ],\n305\t                |row| row.get(0),\n306\t            )\n307\t            .ok();\n308\t        Ok(version)\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    async fn node_settings_version_set(\n312\t have an 'index' field anymore\n488\t        Ok(())\n489\t    }\n490\t\n491\t    async fn idempotency_check(&self, key: &str) -> Result<Option<IdempotencyEntry>> {\n492\t        let result: }\n506\t\n507\t    async fn idempotency_record(&self, entry: &IdempotencyEntry) -> Result<()> {\n508\t        self.execute(\n509\t            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO idempotency_cache (key, &entry.body_sha256 as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n514\t                &entry.miroir_task_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n515\t                &entry.expires_at as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n516\t            ] as u64)\n527\t    }\n528\t\n529\t    async fn job_enqueue(&self, job: &Job) -> Result<()> {\n530\t        self.execute(\n531\t            \"INSERT INTO jobs (id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress)\n532\t             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7)\",\n533\t            &[\n534\t                &job.id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n535\t                &job.job_type,\n536\t                &job.params,\n537\t                &job.state.to_string(),\n538\t                &job.claimed_by.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n539\t                &job.claim_expires_at.map(|v| v as i64).unwrap_or(0),\n540\t                &job.progress,\n541\t            ] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n542\t        )?;\n543\t        Ok(())\n544\t    }\n545\t\n546\t    async fn job_dequeue(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Job>> {\n547\t        // Start a transaction\n548\t        let conn = self\n549\t            .conn\n550\t            .lock()\n551\t            .map_err(|e| TaskStoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;\n552\t        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n553\t\n554\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis() as u64;\n555\t        let expires_at = now + (5 * 60 * 1000); // 5 minutes from now\n556\t\n557\t        // Find and claim a job\n558\t        let job_id: Option<String> = tx\n559\t            .query_row(\n560\t                \"SELECT id FROM jobs WHERE state = 'queued' ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1\",\n561\t                [],\n562\t                |row| row.get(0),\n563\t            )\n564\t            .ok();\n565\t\n566\t        if let Some(ref job_id) = job_id {\n567\t            tx.execute(\n568\t                \"UPDATE jobs SET state = 'in_progress', claimed_by = ?1, claim_expires_at = ?2 WHERE id = ?3\",\n569\t                [\n570\t                    &worker_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n571\t                    &expires_at as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n572\t                    job_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n573\t                ],\n574\t            )?;\n575\t\n576\t            // Fetch the updated job\n577\t            let job: Job = tx.query_row(\n578\t                \"SELECT id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress\n579\t                 FROM jobs WHERE id = ?1\",\n580\t                [job_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n581\t                |row| {\n582\t                    Ok(Job {\n583\t                        id: row.get(0)?,\n584\t                        job_type: row.get(1)?,\n585\t                        params: row.get(2)?,\n586\t                        state: row.get::<_, String>(3)?.parse().map_err(parse_error)?,\n587\t                        claimed_by: {\n588\t                            let claimed: String = row.get(4)?;\n589\t                            if claimed.is_empty() {\n590\t                                None\n591\t                            } else {\n592\t                                Some(claimed)\n593\t                            }\n594\t                        },\n595\t                        claim_expires_at: {\n596\t                            let expires: i64 = row.get(5)?;\n597\t                            if expires == 0 {\n598\t                                None\n599\t                            } else {\n600\t                                Some(expires as u64)\n601\t                            }\n602\t                        },\n603\t                        progress: row.get(6)?,\n604\t                    })\n605\t                },\n606\t            )?;\n607\t\n608\t            tx.commit()?;\n609\t            Ok(Some(job))\n610\t        } else {\n611\t            tx.commit()?;\n612\t            Ok(None)\n613\t        }\n614\t    }\n615\t\n616\t    async fn job_update_status(\n617\t        &self,\n618\t        job_id: &str,\n619\t        status: JobState,\n620\t        result: Option<&str>,\n621\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n622\t        self.execute(\n623\t            \"UPDATE jobs SET state = ?1, progress = ?2 WHERE id = ?3\",\n624\t            &[\n625\t                &status.to_string(),\n626\t                &result.unwrap_or(\"\").to_string(),\n627\t                &job_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n628\t            ],\n629\t        )?;\n630\t        Ok(())\n631\t    }\n632\t\n633\t    async fn job_get(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Job>> {\n634\t        let result: Option<Job> = self\n635\t            .query_row(\n636\t                \"SELECT id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress\n637\t                 FROM jobs WHERE id = ?1\",\n638\t                &[&job_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n639\t                |row| {\n640\t                    Ok(Job {\n641\t                        id: row.get(0)?,\n642\t                        job_type: row.get(1)?,\n643\t                        params: row.get(2)?,\n644\t                        state: row.get::<_, String>(3)?.parse().map_err(parse_error)?,\n645\t                        claimed_by: {\n646\t                            let claimed: String = row.get(4)?;\n647\t                            if claimed.is_empty() {\n648\t                                None\n649\t                            } else {\n650\t                                Some(claimed)\n651\t                            }\n652\t                        },\n653\t                        claim_expires_at: {\n654\t                            let expires: i64 = row.get(5)?;\n655\t                            if expires == 0 {\n656\t                                None\n657\t                            } else {\n658\t                                Some(expires as u64)\n659\t                            }\n660\t                        },\n661\t                        progress: row.get(6)?,\n662\t                    })\n663\t                },\n664\t            )\n665\t            .ok();\n666\t        Ok(result)\n667\t    }\n668\t\n669\t    async fn job_list(&self, status: Option<JobState>, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Job>> {\n670\t        let mut sql =\n671\t            \"SELECT id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress FROM jobs\"\n672\t                .to_string();\n673\t\n674\t        if status.is_some() {\n675\t            sql.push_str(\" WHERE state = ?\");\n676\t        }\n677\t\n678\t        sql.push_str(&format!(\" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT {limit}\"));\n679\t\n680\t        let status_str: Option<String> = status.map(|s| s.to_string());\n681\t        let params: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = match &status_str {\n682\t            Some(s) => vec![s],\n683\t            None => vec![],\n684\t        };\n685\t\n686\t        self.query_map(&sql, &params, |row| {\n687\t            Ok(Job {\n688\t                id: row.get(0)?,\n689\t                job_type: row.get(1)?,\n690\t                params: row.get(2)?,\n691\t                state: row.get::<_, String>(3)?.parse().map_err(parse_error)?,\n692\t                claimed_by: {\n693\t                    let claimed: String = row.get(4)?;\n694\t                    if claimed.is_empty() {\n695\t                        None\n696\t                    } else {\n697\t                        Some(claimed)\n698\t                    }\n699\t                },\n700\t                claim_expires_at: {\n701\t                    let expires: i64 = row.get(5)?;\n702\t                    if expires == 0 {\n703\t                        None\n704\t                    } else {\n705\t                        Some(expires as u64)\n706\t                    }\n707\t                },\n708\t                progress: row.get(6)?,\n709\t            })\n710\t        })\n711\t    }\n712\t\n713\t    async fn leader_lease_acquire(&self, lease: &LeaderLease) -> Result<bool> {\n714\t        let conn = self\n715\t            .conn\n716\t            .lock()\n717\t            .map_err(|e| TaskStoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;\n718\t        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n719\t\n720\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis() as u64;\n721\t\n722\t        // Check if there's an existing valid lease for this scope\n723\t        let existing: Option<(String, u64)> = tx\n724\t            .query_row(\n725\t                \"SELECT scope, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1 AND expires_at > ?2\",\n726\t                [&lease.scope as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql, &now as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n727\t                |row| Ok((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?)),\n728\t            )\n729\t            .ok();\n730\t\n731\t        let acquired = if existing.is_some() {\n732\t            false\n733\t        } else {\n734\t            tx.execute(\n735\t                \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO leader_lease (scope, holder, expires_at)\n736\t                 VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n737\t                [\n738\t                    &lease.scope as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n739\t                    &lease.holder,\n740\t                    &lease.expires_at,\n741\t                ],\n742\t            )?;\n743\t            true\n744\t        };\n745\t\n746\t        tx.commit()?;\n747\t        Ok(acquired)\n748\t    }\n749\t\n750\t    async fn leader_lease_release(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<()> {\n751\t        self.execute(\n752\t            \"DELETE FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n753\t            &[&scope as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n754\t        )?;\n755\t        Ok(())\n756\t    }\n757\t\n758\t rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(ParseError(e.to_string())))\n}\n\n/// Wrapper for String errors to implement std::error::Error.\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct ParseError(String);\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for ParseError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.0)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for ParseError {}\n\n/// SQLite task store implementation.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct SqliteTaskStore {\n    conn: Arc<Mutex<Connection>>,\n}\n\nimpl SqliteTaskStore {\n    /// Create a new SQLite task store.\n    pub async fn new<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<Self> {\n        let conn = Connection::open(path)?;\n        let store = Self {\n            conn: Arc::new(Mutex::new(conn)),\n        };\n        Ok(store)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a SQL statement with parameters.\n    fn execute(&self, sql: &str, params: &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql]) -> Result<usize> {\n        let conn = self\n            .conn\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|e| TaskStoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;\n        conn.execute(sql, params).map_err(Into::into)\n    }\n\n    /// Query a single row.\n    fn query_row<T, F>(&self, sql: &str, params: &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql], f: F) -> Result<T>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&rusqlite::Row) -> rusqlite::Result<T>,\n    {\n        let conn = self\n            .conn\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|e| TaskStoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;\n        conn.query_row(sql, params, f).map_err(Into::into)\n    }\n\n    /// Prepare and execute a query, returning all rows.\n    fn query_map<T, F>(&self, sql: &str, params: &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql], f: F) -> Result<Vec<T>>\n    where\n        F: FnMut(&rusqlite::Row) -> rusqlite::Result<T>,\n    {\n        let conn = self\n            .conn\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|e| TaskStoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(sql)?;\n        let rows: std::result::Result<Vec<_>, _> = stmt.query_map(params, f)?.collect();\n        Ok(rows?)\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl TaskStore for SqliteTaskStore {\n    async fn initialize(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self\n            .conn\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|e| TaskStoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        // Enable WAL mode for better concurrency\n        // Use query_row because PRAGMA journal_mode returns the new mode\n        let _mode: String = conn.query_row(\n            \"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\",\n            &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n            |row| row.get(0),\n        )?;\n\n        // Set busy timeout to avoid deadlock on concurrent writes\n        // Use query_row because PRAGMA busy_timeout returns the value that was set\n        let _timeout: i64 = conn.query_row(\n            \"PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000\",\n            &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n            |row| row.get(0),\n        )?;\n\n        // Create schema_version table\n        conn.execute(\n            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (\n                version INTEGER NOT NULL\n            )\",\n            &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n\n        // Check current version\n        let current_version: Option<i64> = conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT version FROM schema_version\",\n                &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n                |row| row.get(0),\n            )\n            .ok();\n\n        if current_version.is_none() {\n            // Initialize schema\n            Self::init_schema(&conn)?;\n            conn.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (1)\",\n                &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n            )?;\n        } else if current_version != Some(SCHEMA_VERSION) {\n            return Err(TaskStoreError::InvalidData(format!(\n                \"schema version mismatch: expected {}, got {}\",\n                SCHEMA_VERSION,\n                current_version.unwrap()\n            )));\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn schema_version(&self) -> Result<i64> {\n        self.query_row(\n            \"SELECT version FROM schema_version\",\n            &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n            |row| row.get(0),\n        )\n    }\n\n    async fn task_insert(&self, task: &Task) -> Result<()> {\n        let node_tasks_json = serde_json::to_string(&task.node_tasks)?;\n        self.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO tasks (miroir_id, created_at, status, node_tasks, error)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n            &[\n                &task.miroir_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                &task.created_at,\n                &task.status.to_string(),\n                &node_tasks_json,\n                &task.error.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n            ] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn task_get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Task>> {\n        let result: Option<Task> = self\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT miroir_id, created_at,                  parse_error(e)\n                        })?,\n                        node_tasks,\n                        error: {\n                            let error: String = row.get(4)?;\n Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    async fn task_update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n            \"UPDATE tasks SET status = ?1 WHERE miroir_id = async fn task_update_node(&self, miroir_id: &str, node_id: &str, task_uid: u64) -> Result<()> {\n        // Get the task, update node_tasks (store only task_uid), and write back\n        let mut task = self\n            .task_get(miroir_id)\n            .await?\n            .ok_or_else(|| TaskStoreError::NotFound(miroir_id.to_string()))?;\n        task.node_tasks.insert(node_id.to_string(),            &node_tasks_json as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                &miroir_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n            ],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn task_list(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<Task>> {\n        let mut sql =\n            \"SELECT miroir_id, created_at, status, node_tasks, error FROM tasks\".to_string();\n        let mut params = Vec::new();\n        let mut wheres = Vec::new();\n\n        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n            wheres.push(\"status = ?\".to_string());\n            params.push(status.to_string());\n        }\n\n        if !wheres.is_empty() {\n            sql.push_str(\" WHERE \");\n            sql.push_str(&wheres.join(\" AND \"));\n        }\n\n        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY created_at DESC\");\n\n        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {limit}\"));\n        }\n\n        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" OFFSET {offset}\"));\n        }\n\n        let params_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> =\n            params.iter().map(|s| s as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n\n        self.query_map(&sql, &params_refs, rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n            Ok(Task {\n                miroir_id: row.get(0)?,\n                created_at: row.get(1)?,\n                status: row.get::<_, String>(2)?.parse().map_err(parse_error)?,\n                node_tasks,\n                error: {\n                    let error: String = row.get(4)?;\n                    if error.is_empty() {\n                        None\n                    } else {\n                        Some(error)\n                    }\n                },\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn node_settings_version (index_uid, node_id, version, updated_at)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4)\",\n            &[\n                &index as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                &node_id as &alias.created_at,\n                &history_json,\n            ] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn alias_get(&self, name: &str) -> Result<Option<Alias>> {\n        let result: Option<Alias> = self\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT name, kind, current_uid, target_uids, version, created_at, history\n                 FROM aliases WHERE single-target aliases\n                    let target_uids: Option<Vec<String>> = if target_uids_json == \"null\" || target_uids_json.is_empty() {\n                        None\n                    }                 serde_json::from_str(&history_json)\n                            .map_err(|e| rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n                    Ok(Alias {\n          }\n                        },\n                        target_uids,\n                        version: row.get(4)?,\n                        created_at: row.get(5)?,\n                        history,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .ok();\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    async fn alias_delete(&self, name: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM aliases WHERE name = ?1\",\n            &[&name as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn alias_list(&self) -> Result<Vec<Alias>> {\n        self.query_map(\n            \"SELECT name, kind, current_uid, target_uids, version, created_at, history FROM aliases\",\n            &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n            |row| {\n                let target_uids_json: String = row.get(3)?;\n                // Handle null case for single-target aliases\n                let target_uids: Option<Vec<String>> = if target_uids_json == \"null\" || target_uids_json.is_empty() {\n                    None\n                } else {\n                    let parsed: Vec<String> = serde_json::from_str(&target_uids_json).map_err(|e| rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n                    Some(parsed)\n                };\n                let history_json: String = row.get(6)?;\n                let history: Vec<super::schema::AliasHistoryEntry> =\n                    serde_json::from_str(&history_json)\n                        .map_err(|e| rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;\n                Ok(Alias {\n                    name: row.get(0)?,\n                    kind: row.get::<_, String>(1)?.parse().map_err(|e| {\n                        parse_error(e)\n                    })?,\n                    current_uid: {\n                        let uid: String = row.get(2)?;\n                        if uid.is_empty() { None } else { Some(uid) }\n                    },\n                    target_uids,\n                    version: row.get(4)?,\n                    created_at: row.get(5)?,\n                    history,\n                })\n            },\n        )\n    }\n\n    async fn session_upsert(&self, session: &Session) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO sessions (session_id, last_write_mtask_id, last_write_at, pinned_group, min_settings_version, ttl)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6)\",\n            &[\n                &session.session_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                &session.last_write_mtask_id.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                &session.last_write_at.map(|v| v as i64).unwrap_or(0),\n                &session.pinned_group,\n                &session.min_settings_version,\n                &(session.ttl as i64),\n            ] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn session_get(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Session>> {\n        let result: Option<Session> = self\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT session_id, last_write_mtask_id, last_write_at, pinned_group, min_settings_version, ttl\n                 FROM sessions WHERE session_id = ?1\",\n                &[&session_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(Session {\n                        session_id: row.get(0)?,\n                        last_write_mtask_id: {\n                            let id: String = row.get(1)?;\n                            if id.is_empty() { None } else { Some(id) }\n                        },\n                        last_write_at: {\n                            let at: i64 = row.get(2)?;\n                            if at == 0 { None } else { Some(at as u64) }\n                        },\n                        pinned_group: row.get(3)?,\n                        min_settings_version: row.get(4)?,\n                        ttl: row.get(5)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .ok();\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    async fn session_delete(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE session_id = ?1\",\n            &[&session_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn session_delete_by_index(&self, _index: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        // This method is no longer applicable with the new schema\n        // as sessions don't have an 'index' field anymore\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn idempotency_check(&self, key: &str) -> Result<Option<IdempotencyEntry>> {\n        let result: Option<IdempotencyEntry> = self\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT key, body_sha256, miroir_task_id, expires_at FROM idempotency_cache WHERE key = ?1\",\n                &[&key as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n                |row| Ok(IdempotencyEntry {\n                    key: row.get(0)?,\n                    body_sha256: row.get(1)?,\n                    miroir_task_id: row.get(2)?,\n                    expires_at: row.get(3)?,\n                }),\n            )\n            .ok();\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    async fn idempotency_record(&self, entry: &IdempotencyEntry) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO idempotency_cache (key, body_sha256, miroir_task_id, expires_at)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4)\",\n            &[\n                &entry.key as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                &entry.body_sha256 as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                &entry.miroir_task_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                &entry.expires_at as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n            ] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn idempotency_prune(&self, before_ts: u64) -> Result<u64> {\n        let count = self.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM idempotency_cache WHERE expires_at < ?1\",\n            &[&before_ts as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(count as u64)\n    }\n\n    async fn job_enqueue(&self, job: &Job) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO jobs (id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7)\",\n            &[\n                &job.id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                &job.job_type,\n                &job.params,\n                &job.state.to_string(),\n                &job.claimed_by.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n                &job.claim_expires_at.map(|v| v as i64).unwrap_or(0),\n                &job.progress,\n            ] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn job_dequeue(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Job>> {\n        // Start a transaction\n        let conn = self\n            .conn\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|e| TaskStoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;\n        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis() as u64;\n        let expires_at = now + (5 * 60 * 1000); // 5 minutes from now\n\n        // Find and claim a job\n        let job_id: Option<String> = tx\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT id FROM jobs WHERE state = 'queued' ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1\",\n                [],\n                |row| row.get(0),\n            )\n            .ok();\n\n        if let Some(ref job_id) = job_id {\n            tx.execute(\n                \"UPDATE jobs SET state = 'in_progress', claimed_by = ?1, claim_expires_at = ?2 WHERE id = ?3\",\n                [\n                    &worker_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                    &expires_at as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                    job_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                ],\n            )?;\n\n            // Fetch the updated job\n            let job: Job = tx.query_row(\n                \"SELECT id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress\n                 FROM jobs WHERE id = ?1\",\n                [job_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(Job {\n                        id: row.get(0)?,\n                        job_type: row.get(1)?,\n                        params: row.get(2)?,\n                        state: row.get::<_, String>(3)?.parse().map_err(parse_error)?,\n                        claimed_by: {\n                            let claimed: String = row.get(4)?;\n                            if claimed.is_empty() {\n                                None\n                            } else {\n                                Some(claimed)\n                            }\n                        },\n                        claim_expires_at: {\n                            let expires: i64 = row.get(5)?;\n                            if expires == 0 {\n                                None\n                            } else {\n                                Some(expires as u64)\n                            }\n                        },\n                        progress: row.get(6)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )?;\n\n            tx.commit()?;\n            Ok(Some(job))\n        } else {\n            tx.commit()?;\n            Ok(None)\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn job_update_status(\n        &self,\n        job_id: &str,\n        status: JobState,\n        result: Option<&str>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n            \"UPDATE jobs SET state = ?1, progress = ?2 WHERE id = ?3\",\n            &[\n                &status.to_string(),\n                &result.unwrap_or(\"\").to_string(),\n                &job_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n            ],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn job_get(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<Job>> {\n        let result: Option<Job> = self\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress\n                 FROM jobs WHERE id = ?1\",\n                &[&job_id as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(Job {\n                        id: row.get(0)?,\n                        job_type: row.get(1)?,\n                        params: row.get(2)?,\n                        state: row.get::<_, String>(3)?.parse().map_err(parse_error)?,\n                        claimed_by: {\n                            let claimed: String = row.get(4)?;\n                            if claimed.is_empty() {\n                                None\n                            } else {\n                                Some(claimed)\n                            }\n                        },\n                        claim_expires_at: {\n                            let expires: i64 = row.get(5)?;\n                            if expires == 0 {\n                                None\n                            } else {\n                                Some(expires as u64)\n                            }\n                        },\n                        progress: row.get(6)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .ok();\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    async fn job_list(&self, status: Option<JobState>, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Job>> {\n        let mut sql =\n            \"SELECT id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress FROM jobs\"\n                .to_string();\n\n        if status.is_some() {\n            sql.push_str(\" WHERE state = ?\");\n        }\n\n        sql.push_str(&format!(\" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT {limit}\"));\n\n        let status_str: Option<String> = status.map(|s| s.to_string());\n        let params: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = match &status_str {\n            Some(s) => vec![s],\n            None => vec![],\n        };\n\n        self.query_map(&sql, &params, |row| {\n            Ok(Job {\n                id: row.get(0)?,\n                job_type: row.get(1)?,\n                params: row.get(2)?,\n                state: row.get::<_, String>(3)?.parse().map_err(parse_error)?,\n                claimed_by: {\n                    let claimed: String = row.get(4)?;\n                    if claimed.is_empty() {\n                        None\n                    } else {\n                        Some(claimed)\n                    }\n                },\n                claim_expires_at: {\n                    let expires: i64 = row.get(5)?;\n                    if expires == 0 {\n                        None\n                    } else {\n                        Some(expires as u64)\n                    }\n                },\n                progress: row.get(6)?,\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn leader_lease_acquire(&self, lease: &LeaderLease) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self\n            .conn\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|e| TaskStoreError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;\n        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis() as u64;\n\n        // Check if there's an existing valid lease for this scope\n        let existing: Option<(String, u64)> = tx\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT scope, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1 AND expires_at > ?2\",\n                [&lease.scope as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql, &now as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n                |row| Ok((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?)),\n            )\n            .ok();\n\n        let acquired = if existing.is_some() {\n            false\n        } else {\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO leader_lease (scope, holder, expires_at)\n                 VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n                [\n                    &lease.scope as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql,\n                    &lease.holder,\n                    &lease.expires_at,\n                ],\n            )?;\n            true\n        };\n\n        tx.commit()?;\n        Ok(acquired)\n    }\n\n    async fn leader_lease_release(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n            &[&scope as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn leader_lease_get(&self) -> Result<Option<LeaderLease>> {\n        let result: Option<LeaderLease> = self\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease LIMIT 1\",\n                &[] as &[&dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(LeaderLease {\n                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .ok();\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    async fn canary_upsert(&self, canary: &Canary) -> Result<()> {\n        self.execute(\n Result<Option<Tenant>> {\n        use sha2::Digest;\n        let mut hasher = sha2::Sha256::new();\n        hasher.update(api_key.as_bytes());\n        let api_key_hash: Vec<u8> = hasher.finalize().to_vec();\n\n        let result: Option<Tenant> = self\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT api_key_hash, tenant_id, group_id\n                 FROM tenant_map WHERE api_key_hash = ?1\",\n                &[&api_key_hash as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(Tenant {\n                        api_key_hash: row.get(0)?,\n                        tenant_id: row.get(1)?,\n                        group_id: row.get(2)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .ok();\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n api_key_hash: Vec<u8> = hasher.finalize().to_vec();\n\n        self.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM tenant_map WHERE api_key_hash = ?1\",\n            &[&api_key_hash as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn tenant_list(&self) -> Result<Vec<Tenant>> {\n        self.query_map(\n            \"SELECT api_key_hash, tenant_id, group_id FROM tenant_map\",\n (session_id, csrf_token, admin_key_hash, created_at, expires_at, revoked, user_agent, source_ip)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7, ?8)\",\n            &[\n assert_eq!(all.len(), 1);\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t#[tokio::test]\n355\tasync fn test_leader_lease() {\n356\t    let store = SqliteTaskStore::new(\":memory:\").await.unwrap();\n357\t    store.initialize().await.unwrap();\n358\t\n359\t    // Acquire a lease with far-future expiration\n360\t    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis() as u64;\n361\t    let lease = LeaderLease {\n362\t        scope: \"test-scope\".to_string(),\n363\t        holder: \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n364\t        expires_at: now + 60_000, // 1 minute from now\n365\t    };\n366\t\n367\t    let acquired = store.leader_lease_acquire(&lease).await.unwrap();\n368\t    assert!(acquired);\n369\t\n370\t    // Get the lease\n371\t    let retrieved = store.leader_lease_get().await.unwrap().unwrap();\n372\t    assert_eq!(retrieved.scope, \"test-scope\");\n373\t    assert_eq!(retrieved.holder, \"pod-1\");\n374\t\n375\t    // Try to acquire again - should fail (lease still valid)\n376\t    let lease2 = LeaderLease {\n377\t        holder: \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n378\t        expires_at: now + 90_000, // 90 seconds from now\n379\t        scope: \"test-scope\".to_string(),\n380\t    };\n381\t    let store.leader_lease_release(\"test-scope\").await.unwrap();\n386\t\n387\t    // Now can acquire again\n388\t    let acquired = store.leader_lease_acquire(&lease2).await.unwrap();\n389\t    assert!(acquired);\n390\t\n391\t    let retrieved = store.leader_lease_get().await.unwrap().unwrap();\n392\t    assert_eq!(retrieved.holder, \"pod-2\");\n393\t}\n394\t\n395\t#[tokio::test]\n396\tasync fn test_concurrent_writes() {\n397\t    let store = std::sync::Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::new(\":memory:\").await.unwrap());\n398\t    store.initialize().await.unwrap();\n399\t\n400\t    // Spawn multiple concurrent writes\n401\t    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10)\n402\t        .map(|i| {\n403\t            let store = std::sync::Arc::clone(&store);\n404\t            tokio::spawn(async move {\n405\t                let task = Task {\n406\t                    miroir_id: format!(\"task-{}\", i),\n407\t                    created_at: 12345 + i as u64,\n408\t                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n409\t                    node_tasks: HashMap::new(),\n410\t                    error: None,\n411\t                };\n412\t                store.task_insert(&task).await.unwrap();\n413\t            })\n414\t        })\n415\t        .collect();\n416\t\n417\t    // Wait for all to complete\n418\t    for handle in handles {\n419\t        handle.await.unwrap();\n420\t    }\n421\t\n422\t    // Verify all tasks were inserted\n423\t    let tasks = store\n424\t        .task_list(&TaskFilter {\n425\t            status: None,\n426\t 10);\n432\t}\n433\t\n434\t#[tokio::test]\n435\tasync fn test_health_check() {\n436\t    let store = SqliteTaskStore::new(\":memory:\").await.unwrap();\n437\t \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n        expires_at: now + 60_000, // 1 minute from now\n    };\n\n    let acquired = store.leader_lease_acquire(&lease).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(acquired);\n\n    // Get the lease\n    let retrieved = store.leader_lease_get().await.unwrap().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(retrieved.scope, \"test-scope\");\n    assert_eq!(retrieved.holder, \"pod-1\");\n\n    // Try to \"test-scope\".to_string(),\n    };\n    let acquired = store.leader_lease_acquire(&lease2).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(!acquired);\n\n    // Release the lease\n handle.await.unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Verify all tasks were inserted\n    let tasks = store\n        .task_list(&TaskFilter {\n            status: None,\n            limit: Some(100),\n Implementation\n385\t\n386\t### Language and runtime\n387\t\n388\t**Rust.** Rationale:\n389\t- Zero-cost async scatter-gather (Tokio task fan-out maps naturally to fan-out patterns)\n390\t- `twox-hash` crate provides the same hash family Meilisearch Enterprise uses — identical results provable\n391\t- Static musl binaries simplify Docker image production (scratch base, no libc)\n392\t- Existing operator toolchain (forge, NEEDLE, ARMOR, CLASP, SIGIL) is Rust — no new CI infrastructure\n393\t\n394\t### Crate layout\n395\t\n396\t```\n397\tmiroir/\n398\t├── Cargo.toml                  (workspace)\n399\t├── crates/\n400\t│   ├── miroir-core/            (library: routing, merging, topology)\n401\t│   │   └── src/\n402\t│   │       ├── router.rs       (rendezvous hash, shard assignment)\n403\t│   │       ├── topology.rs     (node registry, health state machine)\n404\t│   │       ├── scatter.rs      (fan-out logic, covering set builder)\n405\t│   │       ├── merger.rs
